ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sizes" (
  "size_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each size */,
  "size_label" TEXT /* Label representing the size (e.g., XS, S, M, L, XL) */,
  "chest_width" TEXT /* Measurement of chest width in inches */,
  "shirt_length" TEXT /* Measurement of shirt length in inches */,
  "sleeve_length" TEXT /* Measurement of sleeve length in inches */,
  "waist_size" TEXT /* Measurement of waist size in inches */,
  "hip_size" TEXT /* Measurement of hip size in inches */,
  "shoulder_width" TEXT /* Measurement of shoulder width in inches */,
  "inseam_length" TEXT /* Measurement of inseam length in inches */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Recommended age group for the size */,
  "color" TEXT /* Color of the size measurements */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the size was added */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the size was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("size_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the clothing product */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category to which the product belongs */,
  "size_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the size of the product */,
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* ID of the supplier of the product */,
  "collection_name" TEXT /* Name of the collection to which the product belongs */,
  "material" TEXT /* Material composition of the product */,
  "care_instructions" TEXT /* Care instructions for the product */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the product */,
  "stock_quantity" INTEGER /* Current stock quantity of the product */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the product was added */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the product was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_products_supplier_id FOREIGN KEY ("supplier_id") REFERENCES suppliers ("supplier_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_products_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_products_size_id FOREIGN KEY ("size_id") REFERENCES sizes ("size_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the clothing category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent category (for hierarchical categories) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the category was added */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the category was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inventory_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each inventory log entry */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product that the log refers to */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where the change occurred */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (e.g., addition, subtraction) */,
  "change_amount" INTEGER /* Amount of change in stock quantity */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change occurred */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the log entry was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_logs_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user's role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., admin, staff) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Defined permissions for the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the role was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "suppliers" (
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each supplier */,
  "supplier_name" TEXT /* Name of the supplier */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the supplier */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the supplier */,
  "website" TEXT /* Website of the supplier */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the supplier was added */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the supplier was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("supplier_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "supplier_products" (
  "supplier_product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each supplier-product relationship */,
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* ID of the supplier providing the product */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product supplied by the supplier */,
  "cost_price" REAL /* Cost price of the product from the supplier */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the supplier-product relationship was established */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the supplier-product relationship was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("supplier_product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_supplier_products_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_supplier_products_supplier_id FOREIGN KEY ("supplier_id") REFERENCES suppliers ("supplier_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sales" (
  "sale_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sale transaction */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product sold */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the sale */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where the sale occurred */,
  "sale_date" TEXT /* Date of the sale */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity sold in the transaction */,
  "total_amount" REAL /* Total amount earned from the sale */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the sale was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sale_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)
